title: Verilator thinks code contains delayed assignment for large arrays
body:
I'm running Verilator as a linter.

In my code I have a state array with 256 bytes declared as:
  reg [7 : 0]  state_iaddr;

The state array is reset using a for loop:

  always @ (posedge clk)
    begin: reg_update
      integer i;

      if (!reset_n) begin
        for (i = 0 ; i < 256 ; i = i + 1) begin
          state[i] <= 8'h0;
        end
...
...

But when linting I get the following error message:

verilator +1364-2001ext+ --lint-only -Wall -Wno-fatal -Wno-DECLFILENAME ../src/rtl/rc4_core.v
%Error-BLKLOOPINIT: ../src/rtl/rc4_core.v:123:20: Unsupported: Delayed assignment to array inside for loops (non-delayed is ok - see docs)
  123 |           state[i] <= 8'h0;
      |                    ^~

By varying the loop exit condition (i < X), I can see that when the X is 64 or smaller, Verilator does not respond with the error. But if the loop exit condition is 65 and larger (up to the array size), I get the error message above. I believe that this is an incorrect response from Verilator.

I'm using 'verilator --version': "Verilator 4.200 2021-03-12 rev v4.110-43-g5022e81af (mod)"
